what_input_feature: Some chunk header in the PNG stream must place the 4-byte ASCII FOURCC 'sPLT' (0x73 0x50 0x4c 0x54) into chunk_name so the 32-bit equality `chunk_name == png_sPLT` at pngread.c:229 becomes true. The required content is a fixed multi-byte literal at a structurally-determined offset (the chunk-type field following the 4-byte length).
why_winner_satisfies: Both winning fuzzers (cmplog, value_profile) clear FOURCC equality CMPs that gate other chunk handlers in the same dispatch chain — the SOURCE CONTEXT overlay shows W-only hits on png_PLTE, png_eXIf, png_iCCP, png_tRNS, png_zTXt handler call sites, and DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows W reaches the true side of those equality tests (e.g. line 159 T=5 vs L T=0; line 229 T=28 vs L T=0). Each individual CMP is the same 4-byte-magic shape as png_sPLT, so the fuzzers that solved the sibling chunks also solve this one.
why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP introspection: it produces seeds whose offset-0x35-0x38 chunk-type field is restricted to byte sets {0x73,0x59,0x47,0x42} matching only sRGB / sYGB variants (see BYTE DIFF), never randomly hitting the 32-bit constant 0x73504c54 in 12h of search. The DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows L takes the true side ZERO times at every chunk-FOURCC equality test on the chain (lines 159, 179, 224, 229, 244, 249) — naive fails the whole family of 4-byte FOURCC gates, not just sPLT.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog>naive (I2S): I2SRandReplace intercepts the 32-bit CMP `chunk_name == png_sPLT`, records the constant operand 0x73504c54 in CmpLogObserver, and a subsequent mutation substitutes that literal at byte positions in the input — the I2S-floor (dash-row) ancestor confirmed in lineage of seed 0249d0124e1ec000 at depth 6 is direct evidence. value_profile>naive (value_profile): the same 32-bit CMP feeds CMP_MAP with a Hamming-distance bucket; inputs whose chunk-name field shares 1, 2, or 3 bytes with `sPLT` add new CMP_MAP buckets and are preserved, providing a gradient ladder that bridges to the exact match. Both techniques attack the same single input feature (a 4-byte FOURCC literal) but via complementary mechanisms — hence single_feature, multi-mechanism.
